name: COURIER
subject: When Bishop Budde preached mercy, the far right attacked
body:
At Tuesday’s National Cathedral inaugural prayer service, Episcopal Bishop Mariann Budde stood before Donald Trump, J.D. Vance, and their families and made a plea for mercy.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100954949/524473297/2130526953?refcode=emwrapper&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMDU4MzZmM2QtMzFkYi1lZjExLTg4ZjgtMDAyMjQ4MmE5NTc5IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=1oa8xrbs3oc3GtKOYnjO3GNJ50NS406lXzROfXu7IZk=&emci=ca6f02cb-17db-ef11-88f8-0022482a9579&emdi=05836f3d-31db-ef11-88f8-0022482a9579&ceid=14016711)
Peter,
At Tuesday’s National Cathedral inaugural prayer service, Episcopal Bishop Mariann Budde stood before Donald Trump, J.D. Vance, and their families and made a plea for mercy.
**She asked Donald Trump to show compassion for LGBTQ kids and immigrant families—many of whom are terrified about what his second term could mean for their lives:**
_"In the name of our God, I ask you to have mercy upon the people in our country who are scared now."_
Bishop Budde’s words were a courageous and honest call for compassion, rooted in the deepest values of the Christian faith: mercy, dignity, and justice for the marginalized. But what happened next showed just how coordinated and effective the far-right media machine has become in silencing dissent and distorting truth.
**[We’ll explain more in a moment, but can you chip in $25 to support the progressive media alternative America desperately needs? Together, we can lift up voices of truth and stand strong against the far right.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100954950/524473299/2081197767?refcode=20250125-EM-Bishop-Budde&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMDU4MzZmM2QtMzFkYi1lZjExLTg4ZjgtMDAyMjQ4MmE5NTc5IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=1oa8xrbs3oc3GtKOYnjO3GNJ50NS406lXzROfXu7IZk=)**
**In the right-wing media, the response to Budde’s plea for compassion was swift, brutal, and orchestrated.**
Donald Trump started the attacks on Truth Social, calling her a “so-called bishop” and accusing her of spreading lies. _Fox News_ and far-right influencers followed his lead, smearing Budde’s character and twisting her words:
* **Sean Hannity** called her sermon “a disgraceful prayer full of fearmongering and division.”
* Far-right influencer **Matt Walsh** claimed she was “blasphemous” and said, “Hell exists for people like Mariann.”
* _Fox’s_ **Greg Gutfeld** went so far as to call her “Satan,” saying she represents “compassion exploited for terrible beliefs."

These attacks are part of a larger strategy by Trump and his allies: intimidate dissenters, and create fear to silence those who dare speak the truth and challenge their agenda.
**And right now, their strategy is working.**
That’s why your support for COURIER is so urgent right now. We’re building a progressive media alternative to counter their lies and lift up the voices they want to silence.
**Where they distort, we tell the truth. Where they bully, we empower.**
But we can’t do any of this without your help.
**We’ve set an urgent goal to raise $50,000 by the end of January to expand our reach and stand up to the far-right’s influence. Your support will help us:**
* Deliver factual, fearless reporting that holds power accountable.
* Push back against right-wing intimidation and propaganda.
* Bring truth directly to the people who need it most.

[**Please, chip in $25 today to support the fight for truth >>>>**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100954951/524473300/2081197767?refcode=20250125-EM-Bishop-Budde&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMDU4MzZmM2QtMzFkYi1lZjExLTg4ZjgtMDAyMjQ4MmE5NTc5IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=1oa8xrbs3oc3GtKOYnjO3GNJ50NS406lXzROfXu7IZk=)
**[Chip in $25 now!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100954952/524473303/-1581427288?refcode=20250125-EM-Bishop-Budde&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMDU4MzZmM2QtMzFkYi1lZjExLTg4ZjgtMDAyMjQ4MmE5NTc5IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=1oa8xrbs3oc3GtKOYnjO3GNJ50NS406lXzROfXu7IZk=)**
Thank you for standing with us in the fight for truth.
In solidarity,
The COURIER Team

Email us: [**info@couriernewsroom.com**](mailto:info@couriernewsroom.com)
[**Become a member! **](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100954956/524473305/1028889122?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMDU4MzZmM2QtMzFkYi1lZjExLTg4ZjgtMDAyMjQ4MmE5NTc5IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=1oa8xrbs3oc3GtKOYnjO3GNJ50NS406lXzROfXu7IZk=&emci=ca6f02cb-17db-ef11-88f8-0022482a9579&emdi=05836f3d-31db-ef11-88f8-0022482a9579&ceid=14016711)

[**Update Your Information**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100954959/524473315/1324474970?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMDU4MzZmM2QtMzFkYi1lZjExLTg4ZjgtMDAyMjQ4MmE5NTc5IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=1oa8xrbs3oc3GtKOYnjO3GNJ50NS406lXzROfXu7IZk=&emci=ca6f02cb-17db-ef11-88f8-0022482a9579&emdi=05836f3d-31db-ef11-88f8-0022482a9579&ceid=14016711)

name: Katie Porter
subject: Hoping to see you on Zoom tomorrow!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Truth to Power PAC
body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/9?t=1&akid=39404%2E3422442%2EKtMhPj)
Derek, it’s not too late to **[join me tomorrow](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ttp-em-202501-zoom?refcode=em20250125-39404&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=39404_3422442_KtMhPj&akid=39404%2E3422442%2EKtMhPj)** for a discussion over Zoom about Trump’s first week in office and what’s ahead.
There’s still time for you to secure your spot, but time is running short—**[so make a donation of any amount to Truth to Power if you’d like to join me tomorrow for this virtual discussion.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ttp-em-202501-zoom?refcode=em20250125-39404&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=39404_3422442_KtMhPj&akid=39404%2E3422442%2EKtMhPj)**
**[A Whiteboard on Trump’s White House](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ttp-em-202501-zoom?refcode=em20250125-39404&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=39404_3422442_KtMhPj&akid=39404%2E3422442%2EKtMhPj)**
Sunday, January 26, 4pm PT/7pm ET
Zoom link provided upon donation of any amount
I hope to see you tomorrow!
Katie Porter
_*After you donate, the zoom link will be sent in your ActBlue receipt. Look for an email from Actblue with the subject line: “Thank you for your contribution!”_

**Katie Porter started Truth to Power to support Democrats who reject corporate PAC money** and are running to protect the environment, restore the nationwide right to an abortion, and create an economy that works for working people.
---
Paid for by Truth to Power PAC
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you. But, if you'd like to receive fewer emails, [**you can do so here**](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/receive-fewer-ttp-emails/). If you need to remove yourself from our email list, [click here to unsubscribe](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/ttp-unsubscribe/?t=2&akid=39404%2E3422442%2EKtMhPj).

To contribute via check, please address to: Truth to Power PAC, PO Box 5176, Irvine, CA 92616

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: One Year of Working to Win Back Our State
body:
Here's to Many More!
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
| | |
---
| | Peter,
_Did you know that the Rocky Mountain Voice is officially one year old this month?_ As we celebrate our one-year anniversary and welcome in a new year, the Rocky Mountain Voice is energized with fresh perspective and initiative.
In just 12 short months, RMV has become a trusted, respected news source for **over 170,000 Coloradans** , filling the gap left by the liberal legacy media. We remain dedicated to expanding our reach and offering information and interaction!
With over 30 citizen journalists, robust social media outreach, and attendance at local events, we’re connecting with voters directly and authentically. This year, we have even bigger plans in store!
Expanding our outreach and expertise to every corner of the state, being the voice to a growing number of grassroots citizens, and delivering hard-hitting stories based in the truth are how RMV will promote our pro-citizen, pro-liberty mission in 2025.
RMV isn’t just about reporting the news; it’s about driving the narrative and inspiring action. Our focus is on celebrating Colorado’s heritage, defending liberty, and empowering citizens to take part in shaping the future.
**But if we want to win back our state, we're going to need to increase our efforts.**
Will you help us win back Colorado? [We're shooting to raise $10,000 for our first anniversary! With our amazing supporters, we know we can achieve our goals to raise funds and turn the tide in Colorado!](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
[Give Now!](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
Thank you for being a part of this mission. It will take all of us if we want to save our state from becoming a woke wasteland!
Together, we will return Colorado to the freedom-loving frontier of the West!
| | |
---
| | | **For Colorado,**
**Heidi Ganahl** **CEO, Rocky Mountain Voice**
---
| | P.S. Help RMV be the voice of truth-seeking Coloradans by making a donation of just $25 today!

---
[Chip in $25!](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
|
---
Follow the Rocky Mountain Voice
| [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---|---|---
|
---

[www.RockyMountainVoice.com](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) 8200 S Quebec St, STE A3601 Centennial, CO 80112

name: The Squad
subject: Donald Trump is wasting no time attacking our democracy
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20250125-1195-foote&t=1001&akid=1195%2E342411%2ENIvc0t ]Squad Victory Fund

Peter,

We’re just a few days into Donald Trump’s second term and his
administration is wasting no time attacking the rights we value.

Here is a glimpse of the damage he’s caused on Day 1 alone:

* Attempted to end birthright citizenship through unconstitutional
means.
* Announced ICE raids targeting major cities like Washington D.C.,
Chicago, and Denver.
* Removed the U.S. from the Paris Climate Agreement — again.
* Removed government websites focused on reproductive health care
resources.
* Pardoned over 1,000 January 6 insurrectionists, including the leaders
of extremist groups like the Oath Keepers and Proud Boys.
* Issued an executive order that recognizes only two genders and
dismisses the rights of transgender and non-binary individuals.

This doesn’t even scratch the surface.

Trump’s actions are a stark reminder of his callousness and cruelty — but
we cannot afford to be disheartened. Now more than ever, we must rally
behind the leaders who are fighting for our values — leaders who represent
the working class, our diverse communities, and the future we deserve.

We need to strengthen the power of our Squad to ensure Alexandria, Ayanna,
Ilhan, and Rashida can continue to lead and advocate for bold, progressive
change.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20250125-movement.MM5k3U&refcode=em20250125-1195&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=1195%2E342411%2ENIvc0t ]We can’t do this work alone,
Peter. If you stand with our
Squad, make a contribution to fuel our work to continue uplifting bold,
progressive leaders like Alexandria, Ayanna, Ilhan, and Rashida.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20250125-movement.MM5k3U&refcode=em20250125-1195&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1021&akid=1195%2E342411%2ENIvc0t ]Chip in ››

Together,

The Squad

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe:

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.squadvictoryfund.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1195%2E342411%2ENIvc0t

Paid for by The Squad Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

In addition to what’s legally required, The Squad Victory Fund does not accept contributions from: (1) corporations, associations, or their PACs; (2) individuals registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA; (3) from the PACs, lobbyists, or SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies; or (4) from the Fraternal Order of Police.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated as follows: 8.33% to each of Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, Rooted in Community Leadership PAC, Politivist PAC, and For the Children PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

To contribute via check, please address to The Squad Victory Fund c/o MBA Consulting Group, PO Box 15845, Washington D.C., 20003

Email us: info@squadvictoryfund.com

I CAN’T IMAGINE LIFE WITHOUT YOU.
Thanks to your support, I’m now President.
And guess what, I’ve already accomplished more in a few days than Sleepy Joe did in 4 years.
No surprise there!
**_At this great moment in our nation’s history, I want to share what we’ll accomplish in the next few days:_**

* We will END the open borders and STOP THE INVASION!
* We will no longer be a nation in decline. EVERY DAY WE WILL WIN!
* We will drill, baby, drill—ENERGY COSTS WILL PLUMMET!
* We will implement tariffs on imports from countries taking advantage of us. AMERICA IS BACK IN BUSINESS!

This is just the beginning of our fight to Make America Great Again.
Now, I need your help to keep the momentum going. My first Presidential END-OF-MONTH fundraising deadline is approaching, and I need your help RIGHT NOW to reach my goal.
**_[This deadline is non-negotiable. clean_body: Thanks to your support, I’m now President. You’re the reason we will Make America Great Again. | | | | | I CAN’T IMAGINE LIFE WITHOUT YOU. Thanks to your support, I’m now President. And guess what, I’ve already accomplished more in a few days than Sleepy Joe did in 4 years. No surprise there! At this great moment in our nation’s history, I want to share what we’ll accomplish in the next few days: We will END the open borders and STOP THE INVASION! We will no longer be a nation in decline. EVERY DAY WE WILL WIN! We will drill, baby, drill—ENERGY COSTS WILL PLUMMET! We will implement tariffs on imports from countries taking advantage of us. AMERICA IS BACK IN BUSINESS! This is just the beginning of our fight to Make America Great Again. Now, I need your help to keep the momentum going. My first Presidential END-OF-MONTH fundraising deadline is approaching, and I need your help RIGHT NOW to reach my goal. I know with you by my side, WE WILL MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN! # FROM THE DESK OF LARRY KUDLOW
---
| | Reader,**I am pleased to share with you that The New York Sun, one of my daily must-read papers, is now the exclusive publisher of my regular columns.

I invite you to consider joining the Sun to receive unlimited access to my latest commentary, alongside some of the finest American journalism.

**But there's also far more to what the Sun is building.

As you know, journalism isn't what it used to be.

I've been in the business for decades, and never before have I seen this once noble profession so abused. The major papers are no longer on the side of the American people, and we are right to withhold our trust.

The impact on our democracy is considerable. Without real accountability, the worst actors in government and at corporate institutions have free reign, and patriotic, hard-working Americans are being sidelined and ignored.

The antidote is to join the movement to build a better alternative. That's the American way.

**The charge to restore American journalism is being led by the Sun.** A proudly all-American, principled publication that has plenty of room for conservative thought and conservative writers, the Sun has upheld the highest journalistic standards since 1833. That's 18 years before the New York Times published its first number.**My decision for the New York Sun to become the exclusive publisher of my regular columns was not taken lightly.**

I value their work and have been impressed by their team, but I know they have a hell of a battle ahead. They are the David facing many Goliaths.

They will only succeed if we join with them in this grassroots national effort. Joining the Sun costs as little as a penny a day. I encourage you to sign up today. Every single person counts.

body:
As bad as we thought — and worse | [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100957223/524475388/1113648782?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNS8xLzc4OTg3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjI4ZmE2MzU2LTMzZGItZWYxMS04OGY4LTAwMjI0ODJhOTU3OSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=J3xWI_NIvppH7u9ulSx87aus9Bba0BdD16d73Zk0KgY=&emci=0e750de8-cdd9-ef11-88f8-0022482a9579&emdi=28fa6356-33db-ef11-88f8-0022482a9579&ceid=16951753)
---
**Here’s what Trump did on Day One:** ❌ **Pulled the U.S. out of the Paris Climate Agreement** , turning his back on the global fight against climate change.

❌ **Pardoned roughly 1,600 criminal rioters** who stormed the Capitol on Jan. 6th, 2021.

❌ **Froze hiring across the federal government** , gutting essential services and paving the way for corruption.

❌ **Ordered an “America First” energy emergency** , which could allow Big Oil and Gas to pollute without limits.

❌ **Withdrew from the World Health Organization** , undermining global health efforts and leaving us vulnerable to future pandemics. Let’s be clear: These executive actions are only the beginning. **If we don’t fight back now, Trump’s damage to our communities, our planet, and our democracy will be irreversible.** **[Unite the Country is mobilizing to hold Trump accountable to his campaign promises, expose his lies, and ensure that his reckless agenda faces resistance at every turn. clean_body: As bad as we thought — and worse | Here’s what Trump did on Day One: ❌ Pulled the U.S. out of the Paris Climate Agreement , turning his back on the global fight against climate change. ❌ Pardoned roughly 1,600 criminal rioters who stormed the Capitol on Jan. 6th, 2021. ❌ Froze hiring across the federal government , gutting essential services and paving the way for corruption. ❌ Ordered an “America First” energy emergency , which could allow Big Oil and Gas to pollute without limits. ❌ Withdrew from the World Health Organization , undermining global health efforts and leaving us vulnerable to future pandemics. Let’s be clear: These executive actions are only the beginning. name: VoteVets
subject: Secretary Hegseth
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS ACTION FUND
body:
For all our sakes, we wish him the best of luck. We hope he will rise to the occasion. We are not counting on it.
#
Peter — after his confirmation vote, Secretary Hegseth will now lead the Department of Defense.
For all our sakes, we wish him the best of luck. We hope he will rise to the occasion. But we are not counting on it.
Trump put up the most unqualified, unprepared nominee for Secretary of Defense in the history of the office. He did it because he wants someone of like-mind who will bend to his will — not because he’s a Veteran, not because he’s an outsider, not because he will shake things up.
He’s a “Yes Man” at a time when the United States faces the most complex threat environment in generations, and as our democracy itself is on the brink. Nearly every single Republican in the United States Senate gave their approval.
To each of them, know this: **VoteVets is coming for you.**
And to you, Peter, we have a humble request:
[**Make a donation to VoteVets as we mobilize to support strong, principled candidates and take the fight to the cowards who voted to approve Pete Hegseth. We need your help to get the job done.**](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-c4-em-jan25.34Pqeh&refcode=em250125-1-exp&t=1&akid=34113%2E3013755%2E_C1LQJ)

name: AOC
subject: They're relying on us giving up
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
#
What the MAGA movement wants is for all of us to give up and say, “America has gone to hell in a handbasket, and we’re done.”
**No.**
If you are a hard-working, innocent person: **I have got your back**.
If you are just trying to make it in this world: **I’ve got your back**.
If you’re not trying to hurt anybody: **I got your back**.
It’s so important that we affirmatively assert that because, now more than ever, there are a lot of people who don’t know who they are safe with.
But when we speak up, we signal to others that there are people in this world who have not given up.
There are times when we are _not_ in positions of greater power, and sometimes what we are able to do is very limited. But even in times when our hands are tied, **one of the most enormously powerful things we can do is keep the light on**.
Don’t let these people’s bullsh*t get you down. What is so important for us to understand is that as long as we don’t give up, **it’s not over**.
We’re going to keep standing with trans people.
We’re going to keep standing with the LGBT community.
We’re going to keep standing for the working class.
We’re going to keep fighting for a better minimum wage.
We’re going to keep fighting to guarantee healthcare.
They’re relying on us giving up. **Hell no**.
We will not be afraid – because that’s what they want.
We do not have to comply with illegal activity — **even if that illegal activity is coming from the President of the United States.**
[Help us continue the fight with a grassroots contribution today.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250125.dt8NtL&refcode=em20250125-7992-P_FND_VID_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=7992%2E2526796%2EYkxZvD)
**[Contribute $5](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250125.dt8NtL&refcode=em20250125-7992-P_FND_VID_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=7992%2E2526796%2EYkxZvD)**
In solidarity,
Alexandria

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20250125?refcode=em20250125-7992-P_FND_VID_GRM&t=21&refcode2=7992_2526796_YkxZvD&akid=7992%2E2526796%2EYkxZvD)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20250125-7992-P_FND_VID_GRM&t=22&akid=7992%2E2526796%2EYkxZvD)**

name: John Fetterman
subject: we need to protect this fundamental right
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fetterman for PA
body:
Since the day I launched my first campaign, I’ve always stood on the side of Roe and a woman’s right to make her own health care choices.
#
Since the day I launched my first campaign, I’ve always stood on the side of _Roe_ and a woman’s right to make her own health care choices, Derek.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jf-email-20250125?refcode=em20250125-9829&refcode2=9829_434096_78Kj_u&akid=9829%2E434096%2E78Kj_u)
**It’s absurd to mandate criminalization because of those choices** — which is why I proudly voted NO on the so-called _“Born-Alive Survivors Protection Act.”_ 🙅♂️
Right-wing extremists have been hellbent on rolling back abortion access, and Democrats need to do everything we can to protect this fundamental right. **This is non-negotiable for me.**
[**If you’re with me, I could use your support to keep standing up for our rights in the Senate. Please chip in $5 or anything you can to my campaign today.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jf-email-20250125?refcode=em20250125-9829&refcode2=9829_434096_78Kj_u&akid=9829%2E434096%2E78Kj_u)

John ❤️

name: Jason Miyares
subject: How we lower crime
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Miyares for Virginia |
body:
Let’s talk about Operation Ceasefire.| | | Friend, Serving as Attorney General isn’t just about getting justice for victims of crime in the Commonwealth. It’s about developing smart, law and order policies ensuring that there are fewer victims in the first place. Those represent lives saved, and families kept whole. When we focus on keeping the small percentage of individuals committing a disproportionate share of serious violence off the streets, we protect Virginia families and communities. I couldn’t be more proud of the results of this program.

subject: Senate Republicans are already attacking reproductive rights

body:
| | |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100960087/524477923/-1308448140?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk440-fr-nd&recurring=0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYjE3OWM2ZS0zNWRiLWVmMTEtODhmOC0wMDIyNDgyYTk1NzkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DA5qb5jqsSREbeIIMGN-mBly4p3Z71UBuroil6_VGuM=)
Peter,
**We are less than one week into the Trump administration, and Senate Republicans have wasted no time coming after women's reproductive rights.**
**[If you're with me in the fight against the GOP's anti-women, anti-abortion agenda, chip in $5 now.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100960091/524477925/-1308448140?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk440-fr-nd&recurring=0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYjE3OWM2ZS0zNWRiLWVmMTEtODhmOC0wMDIyNDgyYTk1NzkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DA5qb5jqsSREbeIIMGN-mBly4p3Z71UBuroil6_VGuM=)**
Senate Republicans didn't spend this week pushing for legislation to lower the cost of groceries or make health care more affordable like they promised on the campaign trail – nope. They went full steam ahead on advancing their extreme agenda to control women's bodies. The bill the Senate voted on this week – and that Democrats blocked – would have let the federal government override the decisions of families and create new, medically unnecessary barriers for doctors and patients.
Trump and Republicans are so certain that they know better than women and doctors that they are trying to insert the federal government into reproductive health care in the most unthinkable ways. **Republicans may control Congress and the White House – but we can't let them forget that the vast majority of Americans SUPPORT abortion rights.** That's why now more than ever, we need to keep speaking out and fighting back against Trump and Republicans' draconian attempts to control women and their bodies.
I will be doing everything I can in the Senate to be a firewall against Trump and Republicans' attacks on women's freedom – but I need your help. **Will you join me in this fight, Peter? [Can you chip in $5 or more right now to help me hold Trump and Republicans accountable and protect abortion rights?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100960093/524477946/-1308448140?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk440-fr-nd&recurring=0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYjE3OWM2ZS0zNWRiLWVmMTEtODhmOC0wMDIyNDgyYTk1NzkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DA5qb5jqsSREbeIIMGN-mBly4p3Z71UBuroil6_VGuM=)**

## Important Call to Action
When I write, I make an effort to ensure every fact is backed by verifiable data. [Ground News](https://substack.com/redirect/99b8a296-ae39-446f-8c7b-f93699a28acb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is my most invaluable tool for fact-checking. Their app and website compile related articles worldwide, showcasing source bias and factuality.

## This Week's Articles and Podcast
* **Sunday:**[Trump's Mass Immigration Raids Begin](https://substack.com/redirect/4468a7ae-328c-4521-9c55-fd8a2f67193e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg): How to protect your immigrant neighbors, answers to common FAQs, and debunking anti-immigrant propaganda

* **Thursday:**[Federal Govt Bans DEIA--Requires Employees to Snitch On Each Other](https://substack.com/redirect/f093aa3a-258a-4329-a8a2-276eb257de39?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg): Sharing a confidential memo sent to me by multiple Federal Employees

Me: I just fed the baby some salt

Wife: Don't do that, babies don't need that much sodium

Me: Because they're already sodium cute?

body:
| Did you see Bonnie’s important message? Trump’s racist, bigoted actions in his first few days in office cannot be ignored. We need your help to fight back against these attacks on our democracy and civil rights.

**_[Read more from Bonnie below, and please chip in anything you can to show Trump and his extremist MAGA coalition that you’re ready to join the battle with us to protect our rights.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100961704/524481314/305801023?amount=10&source=EM_NBI_BWC_20250125_FR_FirstWeek_6MO_TeamBonnie_NA_NA_FirstWeek2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDY4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYWY5YTQ1Mi0zNmRiLWVmMTEtODhmOC0wMDIyNDgyYTk1NzkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9FRt9r5wzg8jbULc4MFsDTut32uO-nRvqxqahBBnr7M=)_**
* * *
**_Bonnie’s Original Message:_** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100961707/524481316/305801023?amount=10&source=EM_NBI_BWC_20250125_FR_FirstWeek_6MO_TeamBonnie_NA_NA_FirstWeek2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDY4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYWY5YTQ1Mi0zNmRiLWVmMTEtODhmOC0wMDIyNDgyYTk1NzkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9FRt9r5wzg8jbULc4MFsDTut32uO-nRvqxqahBBnr7M=) Trump wasted no time in his first week in office. He’s already begun wreaking havoc on our democracy and civil rights through bigoted, racist action.

Just days ago, Trump reversed the historical 1965 executive order signed by President Lyndon B. Johnson, revoking critical practices of diversity and affirmative action as well as protections for workers from discrimination based on skin color and gender.

President Trump has made it clear what values he’s choosing to lead his new administration with. It’s time for us to establish our values and fight back.

**_[We must raise $10,000 in the next 48 hours. If you stand against Trump’s blatantly racist actions, please chip in $10 or any amount here to help us fight back.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100961710/524481318/305801023?amount=10&source=EM_NBI_BWC_20250125_FR_FirstWeek_6MO_TeamBonnie_NA_NA_FirstWeek2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDY4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYWY5YTQ1Mi0zNmRiLWVmMTEtODhmOC0wMDIyNDgyYTk1NzkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9FRt9r5wzg8jbULc4MFsDTut32uO-nRvqxqahBBnr7M=)_**

LBJ's important order ensured equal opportunity for people of color and women in the federal government. It was a landmark action in the years following Dr. Martin Luther King’s monumental I Have a Dream Speech, whose memory we just celebrated on Monday.

We must honor Dr. King and President Johnson’s work by protecting the rights that we secured decades ago in the face of such flagrant extremism. **Trump’s actions cannot be ignored and we need your help to stand strong.

clean_body: | Did you see Bonnie’s important message? Trump’s racist, bigoted actions in his first few days in office cannot be ignored. We need your help to fight back against these attacks on our democracy and civil rights. = Bonnie’s Original Message: = Trump wasted no time in his first week in office. He’s already begun wreaking havoc on our democracy and civil rights through bigoted, racist action. Just days ago, Trump reversed the historical 1965 executive order signed by President Lyndon B. Johnson, revoking critical practices of diversity and affirmative action as well as protections for workers from discrimination based on skin color and gender. President Trump has made it clear what values he’s choosing to lead his new administration with. It’s time for us to establish our values and fight back. = LBJ's important order ensured equal opportunity for people of color and women in the federal government. It was a landmark action in the years following Dr. Martin Luther King’s monumental I Have a Dream Speech, whose memory we just celebrated on Monday. We must honor Dr. King and President Johnson’s work by protecting the rights that we secured decades ago in the face of such flagrant extremism. name: Tammy Baldwin
subject: Only 5 days in
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE
body:
[ https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/67?t=1001&akid=4568%2E35263%2EgFpUrb ]TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE

Peter,

We’re on Day Five of the second Trump Administration, and so far, I’m
incredibly disappointed by the President’s actions.

Instead of using his first days of the presidency to address the real
issues plaguing millions of Wisconsinites and Americans, like lowering
costs and expanding healthcare access, he prioritized a radical right-wing
agenda.

Already, Trump has come after LGBTQ+ protections, diversity initiatives in
the federal government, reproductive healthcare resources, and more. He
tried to circumvent the Constitution’s 14th Amendment by restricting
birthright citizenship, and he seated multiple billionaires in the front
row of his inauguration.

And Peter, he
issued blanket pardons to thousands of insurrectionists who stormed the
Capitol and attacked law enforcement officials on January 6th, 2021.

This is not what Wisconsinites need right now. They are demanding real
change and relief from rising costs, unaffordable housing and child care,
and so much more.

I’m ready to work with anyone, including President Trump, to deliver for
the people of Wisconsin. I’ll always put the needs of Wisconsinites first,
and I’ll fight for the rights and freedoms under attack by the Trump
Administration.

[ https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/2155?c0=tbe-em-jan2025.tQCIqp&refcode=AU_STB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250125_PM1_V1_S1&t=1004&akid=4568%2E35263%2EgFpUrb ]As I work to make a difference for Wisconsin’s families, please chip in
to uplift my work. We’re certainly going to face challenges ahead in our
efforts to make progress — but I’ll do what it takes to fight for our
state.

clean_body: [ ]TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE Peter, We’re on Day Five of the second Trump Administration, and so far, I’m incredibly disappointed by the President’s actions. Instead of using his first days of the presidency to address the real issues plaguing millions of Wisconsinites and Americans, like lowering costs and expanding healthcare access, he prioritized a radical right-wing agenda. Already, Trump has come after LGBTQ+ protections, diversity initiatives in the federal government, reproductive healthcare resources, and more. He tried to circumvent the Constitution’s 14th Amendment by restricting birthright citizenship, and he seated multiple billionaires in the front row of his inauguration. And Peter, he issued blanket pardons to thousands of insurrectionists who stormed the Capitol and attacked law enforcement officials on January 6th, 2021. This is not what Wisconsinites need right now. They are demanding real change and relief from rising costs, unaffordable housing and child care, and so much more. I’m ready to work with anyone, including President Trump, to deliver for the people of Wisconsin. I’ll always put the needs of Wisconsinites first, and I’ll fight for the rights and freedoms under attack by the Trump Administration. [ ]As I work to make a difference for Wisconsin’s families, please chip in to uplift my work. We’re certainly going to face challenges ahead in our efforts to make progress — but I’ll do what it takes to fight for our state. body:
Once Stefanik is confirmed, she’ll resign her seat in Congress, and the race for the special election will officially begin.
Peter,
Elise Stefanik’s confirmation hearing to be President Trump’s Ambassador to the United Nations was this week.
Once Stefanik is confirmed, she’ll resign her seat in Congress, **_and the race for the special election will officially begin._**
This is a critical moment for our team. When that special election is called, we will have less than 90 days to campaign and reach voters in every corner of this district.
This could be our first chance to fight back and shrink the Republican House majority, so it’s never been more important for us to come together to support Blake and make sure we have strong momentum heading into the special election.
**[Peter, can we count on you to split a donation between our campaign and Heartland Patriots today to help us flip this seat?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/85457?refcode=em20250125-8735&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=8735%2E614464%2E9h0y3K)**

subject: This race is Trump’s nightmare

As a Democrat running to flip this seat, I know we are ready for change. | [View in browser](https://yqlzye.clicks.mlsend.com/tj/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEwODE2MzUsXCJsXCI6MTQ0NTE2ODQ3ODIwMDgxMTQzLFwiclwiOjE0NDUxNjkwNjcxNTQ0ODk1NX0iLCJzIjoiYTU1NWFkOTAzZjQ1MjFmMSJ9)

Team,

Donald Trump and his MAGA allies are TERRIFIED of this race.
Why? Because they know Florida’s 6th Congressional District is their Achilles’ heel. With a razor-thin majority in the House, losing this seat would destroy their plans to push Trump’s Project 2025 agenda through Congress.
**_
As a Democrat running to flip this seat, I know firsthand that this district is ready for a change._**

Book bans. Attacks on reproductive rights. Gutting public schools.

That’s their vision for the next two years— _and it’s up to us to stop it_.

This race is Trump’s nightmare because it’s not just about one seat. A Democratic win here would derail his agenda and show the nation that MAGA extremism is losing its grip on power.

But here’s the thing: they’re pouring dark money into this race, and every dollar is designed to drown out our grassroots movement. They’re betting we won’t fight back.****

|
---
**[Will you prove them wrong? Chip in $25, $50, or whatever you can today to help us flip this seat and deliver the first blow to Trump’s MAGA agenda.](https://yqlzye.clicks.mlsend.com/tj/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEwODE2MzUsXCJsXCI6MTQ0NTE2ODQ3ODMzNzExNzMxLFwiclwiOjE0NDUxNjkwNjcxNTQ0ODk1NX0iLCJzIjoiYjA1YWU1Y2EwMDFjN2FmOCJ9)**

This isn’t just about politics. It’s about protecting our democracy and building a future where everyone has the opportunity to thrive.

Thank you for standing with us. Let’s show Trump that his nightmare is just getting started.

Gratefully,
Josh __

name: Tim Kaine
subject: Pete Hegseth, Russell Vought, Kristi Noem, RFK Jr.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025]
Folks, here’s the latest: Senate Republicans are pushing hard to move quickly on Trump’s Cabinet nominees, as evidenced by the weekend session here in Washington.
https://secure.ngpvan.com/3rUqQASiqUqj_Jp3b_Qy9Q2 [https://secure.ngpvan.com/3rUqQASiqUqj_Jp3b_Qy9Q2]
They pushed through new Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth yesterday and are pressing forward on Kristi Noem, Russell Vought, and RFK Jr.
Senate Democrats are doing everything we can to demand answers from all of Trump’s nominees — and make sure both the voting Senators and the American people know the full truth about who Donald Trump wants to lead critical parts of our government.
For my part, I am committed to demanding answers from the nominees, and I will always vote in the best interest of Virginians and Americans. To be clear — that doesn’t always mean voting against Trump’s nominees. In my 10+ years in the U.S. Senate, I’ve voted to confirm dozens of Cabinet members in Republican administrations. But I will *never* vote to confirm someone that the American people cannot trust.
If you’re with me, I have a simple ask for you today: Will you add your name to my petition to say that our country deserves Cabinet leaders who are trustworthy and honest? [https://secure.ngpvan.com/3rUqQASiqUqj_Jp3b_Qy9Q2]
Thanks for reading and making your voice heard. I’m glad to know you’re with me.
-Tim Kaine
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025]

Team, Donald Trump has given Elon Musk the green light to SLASH government funding -- **and he's setting his sights on PBS.**

For decades, PBS has brought free, impartial, educational programming into homes across the country. From Mr. Rogers to Big Bird to The NewsHour and so much more, it's had an INCALCULABLE positive impact on our society. **But Trump wants cuts that would start closing down PBS stations!**

**We CAN'T just stand by as Trump and Musk take direct aim at PBS,** so we've launched an urgent Live Petition to PROVE Democrats support funding this all-American institution. We need 5,000 more signatures by midnight -- **please, don't delay:**

[](https://go.dscc.org/go/20962?ask=5&t=10&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20250125-UrgentAlert-DOGEDefundPBS-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20250125-UrgentAlert-DOGEDefundPBS-Pros-sl1&akid=19436%2E9716520%2EfjRUjr)

**_Begin forwarded message:_**

We can’t lose momentum now, Peter.

Right now, Republicans have control of the House, the Senate, and the presidency, and we’re bracing ourselves as the incoming administration attempts to dismantle our democratic institutions.

clean_body: | | If you want to help The Collective PAC build Black political power up and down the ballot, and across the country, then you’re in the right place. If you'd like to unsubscribe | | | Peter, Donald Trump has proven once again he’s determined to drag us backward. One of his latest moves? Revoking a Civil Rights-era Equal Employment Opportunity order that has protected workers for over six decades. This isn’t just a policy shift — it’s a direct attack on progress, erasing hard-won protections for workers of color, women, and religious minorities. It’s 60 years of progress being undone in real time. We have fought and won these integral rights before, and now that his administration has propped them up as disposable, we’ve got to take a stand. | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately to The Collective PAC. | | | | | | — The Collective PAC The Collective PAC is strategically focused on creating a truly equitable democracy where our nation’s local, state and federal governments have diverse and talented elected leadership and Black people are fully represented in positions of power to create the policies necessary to progress our communities forward. Since our inception in 2016, we have helped 500 candidates win general elections at the local, state and federal levels. name: Shelby County Republican Party
subject: Shelby County Republican Party News (January 25, 2025)
body:
Wow! What an amazing week this has been!
____
____
Sanity reigns supreme again!____
____
One example of that sanity is that our federal government now officially recognizes that there are only two genders (male and female). Who would have ever thought that something like that would be cause for celebration in the United States of America? But, here we are thanks to the “progress” leftists have made in our nation since Barak Obama declared he was going to “fundamentally transform the United States of America”. We want our nation back!!!____
____
As I write this on Saturday morning, there are 60 executive actions posted on the Whitehouse website that reflect promises made, and promises kept, by President Trump. You can go to<https://www.whitehouse.gov/presidential-actions/> to track his progress in the weeks and months ahead.____
____
The left, as expected, is going nuts! I urge you to not be swayed in your support for the MAGA movement by the left’s predictably deceptive campaign to thwart our efforts to take our country back. Stand firm, stay engaged, support our president, and continue to fight, fight, fight!____
____
Even as we keep our eyes on Washington DC, the primary responsibility of the Shelby County Republican Party starts right here at home in Shelby County. There is a lot of truth to the old adage that “all politics is local”. As such, I want to share a few “local” things with you.____
____
1. Our 200-Club fundraiser is underway for 2025. This is the primary means we use to fund the work we do on behalf of Shelby County Republicans. Attached is the letter and enrollment form we recently sent to our 2024 members. Please consider supporting our efforts by joining our 200-club this year.____

____
2. We have scheduled our Lincoln Day Dinner for the evening of March 20th at the Shelby Oaks Golf Club. Please save that date on your calendars now! Ohio’s Attorney General Dave Yost will be our keynote speaker. We will work hard to maximize the value of this evening for you.____

____
3. If you were at our big Meet the Candidates Night last September, you will likely recall our renewed focus on preparing future generations of conservatives to keep our country great and free! That will be a huge focus of our Lincoln Day Dinner and every event going forward. Please encourage the younger generations, from high school and up, to attend.____

____
4. We have a Primary Election on May 6th and a General Election on November 4th this year, quickly followed by the mid-term election cycle next year. They are all important, but the 2026 midterms are crucial to continuing, and even supercharging, the common-sense changes President Trump is bringing to our nation (and Ohio)! We hope to have every Gubernatorial candidate speak in Shelby County in the next 12 months, starting with AG Yost at our Lincoln Day Dinner. So, please stay tuned and encourage others to do the same.____

____
We have much to celebrate right now, but much work still to do!____
____
We look forward to continued success for our county, our state, and our nation in the weeks and months ahead!____
____
Cheers...Dan Cecil ____
(937) 206-2970 ____
[cecilda.usaf@gmail.com](mailto:cecilda.usaf@gmail.com) ____

You can go to<> to track his progress in the weeks and months ahead. The left, as expected, is going nuts! I urge you to not be swayed in your support for the MAGA movement by the left’s predictably deceptive campaign to thwart our efforts to take our country back. Stand firm, stay engaged, support our president, and continue to fight, fight, fight! Even as we keep our eyes on Washington DC, the primary responsibility of the Shelby County Republican Party starts right here at home in Shelby County. There is a lot of truth to the old adage that “all politics is local”. As such, I want to share a few “local” things with you. 1. Our 200-Club fundraiser is underway for 2025. This is the primary means we use to fund the work we do on behalf of Shelby County Republicans. Attached is the letter and enrollment form we recently sent to our 2024 members. Please consider supporting our efforts by joining our 200-club this year. 2. We have scheduled our Lincoln Day Dinner for the evening of March 20th at the Shelby Oaks Golf Club. Please save that date on your calendars now! Ohio’s Attorney General Dave Yost will be our keynote speaker. We will work hard to maximize the value of this evening for you. 3. If you were at our big Meet the Candidates Night last September, you will likely recall our renewed focus on preparing future generations of conservatives to keep our country great and free! That will be a huge focus of our Lincoln Day Dinner and every event going forward. Please encourage the younger generations, from high school and up, to attend. 4. We have a Primary Election on May 6th and a General Election on November 4th this year, quickly followed by the mid-term election cycle next year. They are all important, but the 2026 midterms are crucial to continuing, and even supercharging, the common-sense changes President Trump is bringing to our nation (and Ohio)! We hope to have every Gubernatorial candidate speak in Shelby County in the next 12 months, starting with AG Yost at our Lincoln Day Dinner. So, please stay tuned and encourage others to do the same. We have much to celebrate right now, but much work still to do! We look forward to continued success for our county, our state, and our nation in the weeks and months ahead! Cheers...Dan Cecil (937) 206-2970 mailto:cecilda.usaf@gmail.com __

# [Steel Tail Feathers](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=155695987&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU1Njk1OTg3LCJpYXQiOjE3Mzc4MjQyODgsImV4cCI6MTc0MDQxNjI4OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.vYGvKWZNS5bfXDxAJph44aC1gnfJVCcraGOrXdcr7gc)
### How the evil empire co-opted cool to destroy American Independence
| | [Lucas Kunce](https://substack.com/@lucastyree)
---
| Jan 25
---
| [](https://substack.com/@lucastyree)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=155695987&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU1Njk1OTg3LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTczNzgyNDI4OCwiZXhwIjoxNzQwNDE2Mjg4LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTg2Nzc0MSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.KNcPdqHsq57iqUPOQcRLCkrv4ov3Dhm4dOr3_vF-2r8&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=155695987&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU1Njk1OTg3LCJpYXQiOjE3Mzc4MjQyODgsImV4cCI6MTc0MDQxNjI4OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.vYGvKWZNS5bfXDxAJph44aC1gnfJVCcraGOrXdcr7gc&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvbHVjYXNrdW5jZS9wL3N0ZWVsLXRhaWwtZmVhdGhlcnM_.DG5C_qaaQhgB0hZgwpuwV8yrvEXp4ytgl6kHV1naUng?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/lucaskunce/p/steel-tail-feathers?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

This week America rejected the next generation of energy in favor of fossil fuels.

Despite the fact that this initiative was pushed by an institutional industrial class of billionaires, the presentation of the whole thing had a flair of contrarianism. Like [rolling coal](https://substack.com/redirect/1111ae5a-b50f-47f3-aaa6-e53c059fcfc5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) at a national level. Rejecting the next generation of energy was framed as if it were the victory of a great rebel alliance rather than what it actually is: embracing a fossil fuel empire focused on maintaining its wealth, power, and influence at the expense of our nation and everyone who lives here.
It was disappointing to see how, here in America, supporting the billionaire class somehow become contrarian cool, so I decided to do a little research into the concept of cool itself.
Lucas's Substack is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9sdWNhc2t1bmNlLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.GjYfozQz1sJh8ccghX8SsWVcY6w5bNTfXnMt1D1x88Y?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=155695987)
I remembered that Matthew McConaughey had rocked some over-the-top cool in Lincoln commercials a few years ago, so I loaded [one up on YouTube](https://substack.com/redirect/f5d9a4b9-a378-477c-9d2b-83ddeaeb8047?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and watched it to see what I could learn.
Matthew McConaughey, the epitome of cool careless confidence, slows to a stop at a railroad crossing, empty rows of seats piled up behind him in his Lincoln Navigator. The shot cuts to his eyes, peering through the windshield, as a train approaches. He joins the rhythm of the rails by drumming the steering wheel with his hands. The crescendo rises as the train arrives and thunders through the crossing. He continues to rock out as the train passes. Finally, the gates open, and Mr. McConaughey flips a switch and laughs to himself. The SUV glides swiftly down the rustic rural road into the great unknown, Mr. McConaughey proudly at the helm. The beautiful gleaming body and rows of dark windows stretched gracefully behind him. A modern American peacock, trailing his long sleek steel tailfeathers for all to see.
And then, I had an epiphany.
A biological theory from the 1970s, called the handicap principle, tries to explain why animals like peacocks, deer, mandrills, and others develop costly and handicapping physical characteristics. It goes something like this:
Animals seek the strongest mates. Therefore, animals have an incentive to cheat to look stronger than they actually are to attract a mate. Because of this, potential mates look for strength markers that cannot be cheated. Handicaps that are costly to develop and that have real consequences for the bearer, such as piles of cumbersome tail feathers, a massive rack of antlers, or bright colors. If an individual can survive and thrive despite squandering massive resources on a flashy encumbrance like those, so the theory goes, then that's the type of strength and survivability a mate would want for its children.
In 1899, a [gilded era with a celebrated mega-rich class quite like the one we currently have](https://substack.com/redirect/4a3c9356-0b46-48b1-b06f-a73f08a1d139?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and well before the handicapping theory was first proposed for animals, an American economist and sociologist named [Thorstein Veblen](https://substack.com/redirect/36d9ce09-6b29-4acf-80a1-e29a1978a408?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) studied similarly squanderous behavior in humans.
He observed that the wealthiest class of people, whom he called the leisure class, demonstrated their wealth and success by spending their money or time wastefully. He labelled the behaviors conspicuous consumption and conspicuous leisure.
Veblen believed that this type of behavior, which he considered terribly inefficient and a failure of capitalism, was a negative and costly drag on societies. And he was worried about the impact on society when normal people tried to mimic the leisure class's conspicuous consumption.
Of course, in Veblen's time, there wasn't much opportunity for normal people to expend massive resources, even in the aggregate, on conspicuous consumption, so the resource intensive acts were mostly isolated to a small group. However, as parts of our society have become more affluent, conspicuous consumption has expanded across the socio-economic spectrum and is now more widely practiced and recognizable.
On the individual level, and setting aside the desire to make moral judgments, a little conspicuous consumption doesn't seem like that big of a deal. If a retired couple wants to spend their money on a six-bedroom seven bath retirement home to heat and cool and live alone in, or a single commuter enjoys spending some of their hard-earned money to drag seven extra seats and an extra ton of metal down the roadway, who are we to judge?

Shoot, as a father of three (soon to be four!) I have a three row Chevy Traverse in the driveway, and I drive it solo a significant amount of the time, just as coolly as Mr. McConaughey. (No, really, just like him, lol)
Not only that but, like my grandfather before me, I've always been a sucker for cool cars. When I was younger I had a Chevy 350 and 700R4 transmission (the same as the Corvette and Camaro) in a 1974 Jaguar sedan and thought it was SO fun to burn people off the line. To get even more power, I rebuilt a massive 4 barrel Holley carburetor I got off eBay and slapped it on top.

Considering how broke I was at the time, these probably weren't the best financial decisions, but the whole point of tailfeathers is that the signal carries a cost! And I was able to carry them, just barely.
But the aggregate weight of the millions of tailfeathers we all carry, cultivate, and nurture is catching up to us. If not individually, then certainly at a national scale. And we need policies that incentivize our country overcoming or shedding that weight, not piling more on.
In the animal world, the highest cost to building, sustaining, and transporting an ostentatious handicap like ornamental feathers or massive antlers is the extra energy required to grow the feature and then haul it around everywhere the animal goes. The same input, energy, is the primary cost of conspicuous consumption in humans as well.
Since we have been talking cars, let's take a closer look at some of the aggregate implications of our perfectly normal individual decisions around what we drive.
In 2023, the top three selling automobiles in America (sorry Mr. McConaughey and Lincoln!), were Ford F-Series, Chevrolet Silverado, and Dodge Ram pickup trucks-- vehicles that have gotten so large that many [don't fit in garages](https://substack.com/redirect/7d43f774-9aec-45c3-b74f-7c16e8de7c9a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) or parking spots anymore.
Just like making a badass rack of antlers, making these modern driving goliaths takes a heavy investment of energy. Some back-of-the-napkin math:
Using the US auto sales numbers of 726,624 F Series trucks (F-150 Lightning excluded), 444,926 Rams, and 543,319 Silverados, and a rough average weight of 5,000 pounds per truck, the total weight of the top three vehicles sold in 2023 comes to about four million metric tons of material.
Uncool sedans like Toyota Corollas, in contrast, weigh about 3,000 pounds each. (Note: I'd use an American made example here, except that I can't, because [apparently US manufacturers weren't making vehicles like that](https://substack.com/redirect/c8809e14-89a3-44bc-9e8f-1fa3b5db17f3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in 2023 and don't like even giving consumers the choice anymore. The closest thing is a Malibu).

So if we swapped out just one year of the top selling trucks for the three thousand pound corolla, as a country we would save around 1.7 million metric tons of material. Using vehicle averages of [60% steel](https://substack.com/redirect/d1498690-f417-4053-834a-ed882796df11?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [10% aluminum](https://substack.com/redirect/92f8dbb3-4d90-4683-95ff-286aace20c5b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), that's a savings of 1.02 million tons of steel and 170,000 metric tons of aluminum.
In energy terms, since it takes about [7,415 kWh](https://substack.com/redirect/71323e4e-9eea-4998-b653-4f56a594afa1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to make a ton of hot rolled steel using a basic oxygen furnace and about [14,500 kWh](https://substack.com/redirect/33ae7684-6b20-4225-9b81-eac1e6af72f9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to make a ton of aluminum, the energy used just to make the additional metal in those 2023 trucks came to 10,028 GWh. From a national perspective, the swap creates enough energy to [power around a million households](https://substack.com/redirect/a706022c-021d-4fff-a890-397c9ba130dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for that entire year.
But that's just the tip of the extra energy iceberg. Let's skip the manufacture, assembly, and shipping part, because that's a lot to figure out on the back of a napkin, and move to the energy cost of moving those millions of tons of extra steel up and down the road every day.
I call it "extra steel" because for the vast majority of us, that's all it is. Sure, in some cases a five seat crew cab, truck bed, and hauling power are needed for everyday work duties. In other cases, some of those things might be needed some of the time. But for most of us, they are largely irrelevant outside the occasional craigslist furniture purchase or backpacking trip.
That's because most people drive to regular old jobs all by themselves. In 2023, the year we have been looking at, [69.2% of working Americans](https://substack.com/redirect/d96f6440-22cb-49f8-8bfa-c536b09c29be?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) drove alone to work every day. The average number of workers per commuting vehicle was just 1.07, meaning that practically all the non-driver seats in any given commuter car, just like McConaughey's Navigator in the Lincoln ad and my Chevy Traverse when I go to work, are empty basically all the time.
On an individual level, depending on your individual circumstances, the cost of buying and then dragging all that extra metal around all the time may not be that big of a deal.

The gas to drive a modern full-size pickup versus a Corolla costs about an extra thousand dollars a year, so $12k or so there on the life of a vehicle, and then there's $25k or so in additional purchase price.
Most people seem to have decided that the individual convenience and feeling of cool and power that comes from looking down from a massive crew cab is worth that extra dollar cost. Just like I did as a young man with my overpowered Chevy 350 - or the powder blue Dodge Ram I had right before it.

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/b9ce7f1c-a172-4c7e-a060-f27e41bc567e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_Me and my best friend Benyam flashing my Dodge Ram tail feathers in 2006_
For our country, however, as Veblen foresaw, the cost of this conspicuous consumption is game changing.
And I'm not just talking about the environment or health or any other so-called granola implications, although they are all at play. I'm talking about national security, American independence, war, and world power.
In 2023, Americans drove [3.263 trillion miles](https://substack.com/redirect/1071869d-cd65-47f6-87a6-5cf9db8760e2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). With 16.6% of vehicles being pickup trucks, and assuming an equal distribution of miles driven, that's 541.7 billion pickup miles consuming 27 billion gallons of gas. Converting just half of those truck miles into Corolla miles would save America 7.74 billion gallons of gas a year.
That same year, we imported [314 million barrels of petroleum](https://substack.com/redirect/0e74a4d2-7c3b-4aea-a700-24d33fcedcf3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from Persian Gulf countries, which comes to roughly 6 billion gallons of gasoline.
In other words, converting half those trucks to Corollas would save more than enough gasoline to finally flip the middle finger to the Middle East forever, to stop bowing down to the whims of authoritarian states like Saudi Arabia, to stop protecting their supply lines with our Navy, to stop going to war there, to reject their grifting and everything else that goes along with the unbalanced relationship we have with the region.
And we could get rid of that dependence, and save trillions in overseas wars and supply chain policing, without even touching Matthew McConaughey's Navigator or a single SUV (like my Traverse, whew!), which there are way more of on the road than pickups.
I suspect that everyone reading this agrees that our goal as a nation should be to enact policies that serve the national interest, not the billionaire class. Policies that put America at the lead or even in charge of the next generation of energy before another authoritarian state, like China, captures that lead and controls international security the way the Saudis and OPEC have over the last several decades.

But we often do the exact opposite. [Obama-era emissions regulations](https://substack.com/redirect/094c2b43-6d28-4eee-902b-f8d6ccddfd0c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) actually _encouraged_ the expansion of vehicle size by making it easier for larger vehicles to pass emission standards. Making the same branded vehicle longer and wider gave it a relatively larger break on emissions, so Detroit (who, of course, lobbied for the changes), made their vehicles bigger. Americans were simply given larger vehicles to purchase whether they wanted it or not.

And as I pointed out with the 2023 American-made car lineup when I couldn't find a 2023 American made Corolla equivalent, Detroit largely stopped making smaller cars altogether, leaving us with few alternatives if we want to support our union brothers and sisters here in America.
The Detroit and oil tycoons also secured and protect a [huge tax incentive](https://substack.com/redirect/ea52f41a-2642-40ea-a83f-82738104b585?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for Americans to buy vehicles over 6,000 pounds. A subsidy for large vehicles so lucrative that [dealers advertise and brag about](https://substack.com/redirect/ed75d5fc-4129-41d3-b1a5-3b1c36cb32c3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) it as it pushes thousands of Americans to buy one of those top 3 vehicles of 2023 that we have been talking about: the Ram, F-150, or the Silverado. Essentially, if you have a small business, even just a side hustle, you can write off the full cost of a vehicle that weights over 6,000 pounds-- it has to weigh over 6,000 pounds-- the first year you own it. For many thousands of people, that might actually make it _cheaper_ up front to buy an F-150 vs Corolla.

And now, this week, at the behest of the industrial class, we have pulled back the only incentives and plans in place to put America first in the next generation of energy and transportation before China secures its place as the world leader and we spend future generations kowtowing to that authoritarian state just like we did the Saudis.

Combine all that with the fact that, as cars have gotten larger, it's hard to feel safe on the road in anything too small anymore, and you have the perfect storm for the situation we are currently in.
Through policy and economics, our country is quite literally digging its own grave.

So, what can we do?
I believe it starts with messaging. Given the evil empire at work here, there has to be a way to tap into America's contrarian streak so that the empire is the empire again and future transportation and energy technology is cool and maverick.
It actually used to be that way.
When I was a kid, the only people who had solar panels were the ones who wanted to stick it to the utility companies because billionaire industrialists were "the man" of that generation that everyone hated. And being contrarian meant getting off the grid.
For years, the only wind turbine that stood in mid-Missouri was outside the New Life Evangelical Center in New Bloomfield, owner of the local Christian TV station, Channel 25 KNLJ. The center was led by its contrarian director, Larry Rice, [who did things like](https://substack.com/redirect/7d035749-9b01-4750-a797-57406d98d8f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) take a group of people into the City Hospital in St. Louis and sleep out front on cardboard with his ten year old kid to persuade the city to let him turn it into a shelter. Or put up crosses on the lawn of City Hall and the state Capitol to draw attention to unhoused people, according to the St. Louis Post-Dispatch.
Now, though, solar panels and wind turbines are perceived as the domain of yuppies and activists, fueled in large part by propaganda campaigns led by the fossil fuel industry. Regardless of the reason, however, the narrative has been lost and the mission of anyone wanting to strengthen our country needs to be figuring out how to flip it back. Why shouldn't grid independence be as cool as a pickup truck?
Interestingly, going back to the pickup vs Corolla discussion from earlier, the $37k in cost difference between the two would be more than enough for basically anyone who makes the switch to obtain utility independence through a solar system and battery storage.
Something that, for most of us here in Missouri, where storms frequently knock out power, would be a lot more useful than being able to haul the occasional Craigslist couch.
An anti-corporate power message rooted in American independence and contrarianism might be our best path to turn this ship around before it is too late.
I'd love to hear what you think and, as always, please share with anyone you think might be interested in what we are talking about here!
Lucas
